Those incidents that resulted in a temporary disability where the individual was unable to work at all (time lost) or was unable to follow their ordinary occupation but instead performed alternate or light duty; and/or put on restricted work/time. An incident is no longer considered temporary should the employee require medical attention beyond the initial assessment, the incident becomes permanent, or should the incident result in fatality.

Please upload your record of indicators for the given year:

a) Frequency index (FI)
The frequency index will be calculated by applying the following formula:
IF = # Injuries x 200,000 / # H M/F worked
Where:
#- Injuries = Number of accidents and occupational or professional illnesses that require medical attention (that require more than one work day), in the period.
#- H M/F worked = Total man/woman hours worked in the organization in a given annual period.
https://www.aguaquito.gob.ec/wp-content/uploads/2018/01/IE-7-REGLAMENTO-DEL-SEGURO-GENERAL-DE-RIESGOS-DEL-TRABAJO.pdf
Please enter your Frequency Index (IF) for the given year.

b) Severity Index (SI)
The severity index will be calculated by applying the following formula:
SI = # days lost x 200,000 / # H M/F worked.
Where:
#- Days lost = Time lost due to injuries (days of employment according to the table, plus the current days of absenteeism in cases of temporary disability).
#- H M/F worked = Total man/woman hours worked in the organization in a given period (annual).
The days of charge will be calculated according to the following table:

Absolute permanent disability (I.P.A.) 6000
Total permanent disability (I.P.T.) 4500

https://www.aguaquito.gob.ec/wp-content/uploads/2018/01/IE-7-REGLAMENTO-DEL-SEGURO-GENERAL-DE-RIESGOS-DEL-TRABAJO.pdf
Please enter your Severity Index (SI) for the given year.

c) Risk rate (TR)
The risk rate will be calculated by applying the following formula:
TR = # days lost / # injuries
or instead:
TR = IG / IF
Where:
IG = severity index
IF = frequency index
https://www.aguaquito.gob.ec/wp-content/uploads/2018/01/IE-7-REGLAMENTO-DEL-SEGURO-GENERAL-DE-RIESGOS-DEL-TRABAJO.pdf
Please enter your Risk rate (TR) for the given year.

Companies registered with the Ecuadorian Social Security Institute (IESS) and compliant with the General Insurance for Occupational Risks, as regulated by IESS, may request the annual Accident Report (Registro de Indicadores de Seguridad y Salud en el Trabajo, SST). This report is issued by IESS under the framework of the General Insurance for Occupational Risks and the national occupational health and safety management regulations.\n\nIf companies miss the January 31 deadline for submitting their required occupational risk management documentation to IESS, they may not be able to obtain the annual Occupational Risk Accident Report (Record of HSE Indicators) for that year. This deadline is strict, and late submissions risk disqualification from receiving the official document.
